_meta.muc_dich: Cay conflict chia theo tung ngach A-E. App random conflict THEO dung ngach dang chon (khong random lung tung). Moi ngach co nhanh rieng.
_meta.cach_dung: Khi chon ngach A (Me gia), engine random 1 case trong nhom A1-A5. Ngach B random trong B1-B10. Moi case la mot 'humiliation/conflict cu the' de AI ke.
_meta.compatibility: Original topic keys are preserved. A structured conflict_catalog is added for reliable app use.
_meta.rule: Never select conflict by free random across themes. Select theme -> subgroup -> conflict item.

A1_bo_roi: the adult child puts the elderly mother in a nursing home against her will to free up the house
the child leaves the mother at a cheap motel during a family trip, too ashamed to bring her along
the mother is not invited to her own grandchild's birthday party
the child changes the locks while the mother is out, forcing her to move
the mother is left alone at the hospital after surgery, no family comes
the child stops visiting entirely after getting wealthy, screens her calls
the mother is dropped off at a senior center and 'forgotten' for the holidays
A2_cuop_tai_san: the child pressures the mother to sell her longtime family home
the child tricks the mother into signing away the house deed
the child quietly drains the mother's pension/retirement savings
the child takes the mother's late husband's life insurance money
the child sells the mother's car without asking
the child forges documents to gain power of attorney over her finances
the child convinces the mother to 'invest' her savings, then keeps it
A3_lam_nhuc: the mother is told to eat in the kitchen, not at the family table
the child won't let the mother hold or babysit the grandchild
the mother is made to sleep in the garage or basement when visiting
the mother is not allowed to attend the child's fancy wedding
the child introduces the mother to friends as 'the help' or nanny
the mother's homemade food is thrown out in front of her
the mother is publicly scolded for her old clothes at an upscale event
A4_loi_dung: the mother is used as free full-time babysitter with no thanks
the mother cooks and cleans daily for the child's family, taken for granted
the mother is left to care for the child's pets and home while they vacation
the mother runs the child's shop/business for free while they take credit
the mother gives up her own life to nurse the family, then is discarded
the child only calls the mother when they need money or a favor
A5_hoi_han: the mother passes away and the child finds letters revealing her sacrifices
the mother quietly disappears/moves away and the child realizes too late
the mother becomes successful or admired by others, shaming the child
a stranger or community helps the mother, exposing the child's neglect
a will/letter reveals the mother secretly paid for the child's success

B_cases: no one at the event recognizes the veteran's service, treating him as a nobody
the veteran is refused a military/senior discount and mocked for asking
the veteran is asked to leave or turned away from a restaurant for his appearance
a security guard/staff searches or profiles the veteran humiliatingly
the veteran can't afford his medication and is dismissed by family
the veteran's war medals are sold or thrown away by a relative
the veteran's own son/grandson is ashamed of him in front of friends
a young person mocks the veteran's service or patriotism to his face
a former comrade or superior betrayed or forgot him
the veteran is overlooked at a memorial or ceremony that should honor him
the veteran is called a liar about his service until proof appears
the veteran is seated at the back or excluded at a family celebration
a wealthy relative belittles the veteran's modest military pension
the veteran is denied entry to an event because he 'doesn't fit in'
the veteran quietly does a humble job and is looked down on for it

C_villain_source: a cold, controlling mother-in-law who treats the bride as staff
a jealous, cruel sister-in-law who sabotages the bride
a snobbish brother-in-law who mocks the bride's humble background
a dismissive father-in-law who never accepts her
a weak husband who won't defend his wife against his family
a manipulative sister-in-law spreading lies about the bride
C_situation: at the wedding itself, the bride is publicly humiliated by the in-laws
on the honeymoon or first family trip, she's excluded and belittled
when she announces her pregnancy, the family reacts with contempt
over family property/inheritance, they treat her as an outsider gold-digger
she's handed servant tasks (apron, serving) in front of guests
she's judged for her job, clothes, or family and made to feel unworthy
she's blamed for something she didn't do to turn the husband against her

D_evidence_modern: a home security camera (Ring/doorbell) captures the truth
GPS location history on a phone reveals where he really was
text messages discovered on a synced device or shared account
an Apple AirTag she finds reveals his movements
credit card and bank statements expose hidden spending
a DNA test reveals a family secret
the smart home (Alexa) recorded a conversation
a shared cloud photo library auto-uploaded incriminating images
the car's built-in GPS/trip log shows a pattern
a fitness tracker's data contradicts his alibi
an email receipt for a gift or hotel she never received
a voicemail or call log that doesn't match his story
D_wife_response: she quietly gathers 14 months of evidence before acting
she secretly refinances/protects the house and assets first
she retains her own lawyer before he suspects anything
she stays calm and lets the truth reveal itself publicly
she documents everything methodically, the way she always kept the books

E_occasion: at a lavish family wedding where the poor relative is treated as an embarrassment
at a funeral where the wealthy side fights over inheritance and excludes them
at a milestone birthday party where they're seated at the back
at Thanksgiving dinner where their humble contribution is mocked
at Christmas where their handmade gifts are ridiculed next to expensive ones
at a summer BBQ/pool party where they're made to feel out of place
at a family reunion where relatives brag and look down on them
at a graduation where the wealthy relatives take credit or ignore them
at an upscale restaurant where their clothes/manners are judged
at a country club event where they're treated as if they don't belong
E_reveal: the 'poor' relative turns out to be quietly wealthy or accomplished
the poor relative was secretly the one funding the family all along
a powerful outsider reveals the poor relative's hidden good deeds
the wealthy relatives' fortune turns out to be fake or crumbling
the poor relative's child/grandchild achieves something that shames them
an old document reveals the poor relative's true sacrifice for the family

the daughter leaves her mother off the emergency-contact list at the assisted-living facility
the son redirects the mother's Social Security deposits into an account she cannot access
the mother is cropped out of the family photograph before it is posted online
the family expects the mother to provide daily childcare while charging her rent for a spare room
the veteran is accused of wearing medals he did not earn during a public ceremony
the veteran's reserved parking space is taken and staff tell him to stop making trouble
a family member hides his service history because it clashes with their polished social image
the bride's homemade wedding contribution is removed and replaced without telling her
the mother-in-law gives the bride a separate list of household rules in front of the family
the husband's family excludes the bride from a major medical decision involving her own spouse
the wife discovers a second lease and utility bills tied to another household
the husband's calendar sync reveals recurring appointments under a false business label
a school emergency form lists another woman as the child's parent
wealthy relatives mock the poor uncle's old truck, not knowing he used it for years of volunteer disaster relief
the poor aunt is seated near the kitchen at a reunion despite having raised two of the successful relatives
the handmade gift is laughed at until its history is explained by the oldest family member
one sibling has done all the caregiving for years while the others visit twice a year
the siblings agree on a care schedule and only one of them ever shows up
a distant sibling sends money and considers the matter settled
the caregiving sibling loses a job and the others say nothing
one sibling moves the parent into their home and the others treat it as convenient
the siblings argue about hiring help while one of them is already doing the work unpaid
the caregiving sibling is accused of living off the parent's pension
a sibling suggests the caregiver has been influencing the parent's decisions
the family demands a full accounting of every dollar the caregiver spent
one sibling hires a lawyer to review what the caregiver has been doing
an accusation of elder financial abuse is made in front of the whole family
the siblings fight over whether to move the parent into assisted living
one sibling overrules a medical decision the caregiver has already made
the siblings disagree about end-of-life care and nobody asks the parent
a sibling who has not visited in years insists on being the one to decide
the family votes on something the caregiver has been handling alone for years
the parent promised the house to the caregiving sibling and the others contest it
one sibling renovates the family home and then claims a larger share
the siblings discover the will names one of them and not the others
a sibling quietly takes items from the family home before anything is settled
the siblings sell the parent's house without telling the one who lived there
the sibling who left decades ago returns when there is something to divide
a sibling reappears at the funeral after years of silence
the estranged sibling asks to be included after the hard part is over
a sibling who was the favorite returns expecting the same standing
a neighbor becomes the real caregiver while the children live far away
a paid caregiver understands the elder better than the biological children do
the mail carrier is the one who notices something is wrong
a church volunteer has been driving them to appointments for three years
a former student stops by every week when no relative does
a step-grandparent with no blood tie is the one the child actually calls
the person named in the will is a friend, not a relative
friends and neighbors are the ones present at the end of life
a chosen family member is not invited to the funeral by the blood relatives
the neighbor who kept the spare key is treated as an outsider when it matters
a trailer park or neighborhood is bought out and the residents are displaced
the community fights a rent increase that will scatter people who have lived there for decades
a longtime resident is pushed out of the only place they know
the town changes and the people who built it are treated as in the way
an old rumor made the whole town misread one person for decades
a quiet donation is misunderstood as something shameful
the community judges someone for a decision they never explained
a person is misjudged because they never defended themselves publicly
someone with no obligation to help is the only one who does
a stranger intervenes when the family will not
a coworker or neighbor speaks up at the moment the family stays silent
the person who helps has nothing to gain by helping

rules: Do not use the same conflict_id during cooldown.
Do not repeat the same subgroup more than twice in the last five stories of one theme.
For theme E, secretly-wealthy reveal weight must remain below 0.20 of published stories.
For theme D, evidence must be concrete and discoverable before confrontation.
For theme C, conflict must remain centered on marriage/in-law power, not generic workplace drama.

F1_ganh_nang_khong_chia: one sibling has done all the caregiving for years while the others visit twice a year
the siblings agree on a care schedule and only one of them ever shows up
a distant sibling sends money and considers the matter settled
the caregiving sibling loses a job and the others say nothing
one sibling moves the parent into their home and the others treat it as convenient
the siblings argue about hiring help while one of them is already doing the work unpaid
F2_to_cao_lam_dung: the caregiving sibling is accused of living off the parent's pension
a sibling suggests the caregiver has been influencing the parent's decisions
the family demands a full accounting of every dollar the caregiver spent
one sibling hires a lawyer to review what the caregiver has been doing
an accusation of elder financial abuse is made in front of the whole family
F3_tranh_quyet_dinh: the siblings fight over whether to move the parent into assisted living
one sibling overrules a medical decision the caregiver has already made
the siblings disagree about end-of-life care and nobody asks the parent
a sibling who has not visited in years insists on being the one to decide
the family votes on something the caregiver has been handling alone for years
F4_tai_san_va_loi_hua: the parent promised the house to the caregiving sibling and the others contest it
one sibling renovates the family home and then claims a larger share
the siblings discover the will names one of them and not the others
a sibling quietly takes items from the family home before anything is settled
the siblings sell the parent's house without telling the one who lived there
F5_cu_cham_tro_ve: the sibling who left decades ago returns when there is something to divide
a sibling reappears at the funeral after years of silence
the estranged sibling asks to be included after the hard part is over
a sibling who was the favorite returns expecting the same standing

G1_nguoi_ngoai_cham_soc: a neighbor becomes the real caregiver while the children live far away
a paid caregiver understands the elder better than the biological children do
the mail carrier is the one who notices something is wrong
a church volunteer has been driving them to appointments for three years
a former student stops by every week when no relative does
G2_gia_dinh_lua_chon: a step-grandparent with no blood tie is the one the child actually calls
the person named in the will is a friend, not a relative
friends and neighbors are the ones present at the end of life
a chosen family member is not invited to the funeral by the blood relatives
the neighbor who kept the spare key is treated as an outsider when it matters
G3_cong_dong_bi_de_doa: a trailer park or neighborhood is bought out and the residents are displaced
the community fights a rent increase that will scatter people who have lived there for decades
a longtime resident is pushed out of the only place they know
the town changes and the people who built it are treated as in the way
G4_hieu_lam_ca_thi_tran: an old rumor made the whole town misread one person for decades
a quiet donation is misunderstood as something shameful
the community judges someone for a decision they never explained
a person is misjudged because they never defended themselves publicly
G5_nguoi_ngoai_dung_len: someone with no obligation to help is the only one who does
a stranger intervenes when the family will not
a coworker or neighbor speaks up at the moment the family stays silent
the person who helps has nothing to gain by helping
